title: Bridge Essay: Mapping brain structure and personality in late adulthood.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Visionary Cosmologies** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Visionary cosmologies — self-transcendence, mystical experience, numinous, awe, cosmic consciousness.

**Mapping brain structure and personality in late adulthood.** — Neuroimage. PMID: 15627574. DOI: 10.1016/j.neuroimage.2004.09.017. Cerebral gray matter (GM) volume decreases in normal aging with aparallel increase in intracranial cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) volume. There is considerable interindividual variation in these changes, and the consequences of age-related GM shrinkage and CSF expansion are unclear. The present study examined whether late adulthood brain structural differences are related to differences in temperament and character.
